videos.title: Why Gold Is At An All Time High
videos.text: Have you seen the price of gold lately? I mean, we're used to gold being valuable, but why is gold currently sitting at all-time highs? The last 2 years have seen a historic bull run for gold with prices jumping well over 100%. Gold is now sitting at over $4,000 an ounce. And to put that into comparison, that's nearly double the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq during the same time period. So, why is it massively outpacing the two largest stock indexes in the world? Is that like gold pays any dividends or delivers anything for shareholder value? Think of it this way. Companies like Apple and Amazon have scaled up to become some of the largest enterprises in history, and yet their stock price has been completely trounced by gold over the last 2 years. But that just begs the question of why a shiny yellow rock is currently one of the top performing assets in the entire world. And the reason is relatively simple. Gold is a store of value, and the huge jump in price is a sign from the marketplace that investors are expecting a surge in inflation, perhaps even beyond what we saw in 2021 and 2022. The fact is is that gold only rises like this when people stop believing in money, specifically fiat currency. Essentially, when people think that central banks have lost control and the dollar, euros, or yen they're holding today will be worth far less tomorrow, that's when they take those currencies and put them into something that can't be printed, like gold. And if you zoom out, that's exactly what's happening right now. Governments are drowning in debt, interest payments are exploding, and central banks are quietly admitting that they can't raise rates high enough to fix it all without bankrupting the governments they serve. So, instead, they're monetizing the debt by essentially inflating the debt away. So, perhaps a better way of looking at it isn't that some yellow rock is going up in value, it's that our currencies are collapsing in real time. And we see that collapse measured by the fact that the price of gold is rising with each passing year. That's why gold is exploding. The meteoric price rise is a vote of no confidence in the global financial system, again specifically fiat currency. And this isn't the first time something like this has happened, either. We saw the same pattern during the Great Depression. I mean, you know, before FDR confiscated everyone's gold and then revalued it in order to pay for his New Deal. We also saw it in the 1970s when inflation spun out of control after we dropped the gold standard. And again in 2008 when money printing went into overdrive. The details change from crisis to crisis, but the bigger picture remains the same. Gold typically doesn't rise when things are healthy. It rises when people are quietly preparing for a world where the promises of government and the value of paper money can no longer be trusted. So, perhaps the most important question is not why is gold going up, the most important question is what happens when the general population finally realizes why it's going up.

See, whatever move gold gives, it always gives a normal move of 10 to $ but above the amount. Right now in the last two to three weeks, the movement of gold is more than 25 to 30 percent to $40 percent, that is unmatchable, meaning before this, till date gold has never moved in this manner, within a single day, the rate of gold has been seen increasing by 10-15,000 rupees. This is an unimaginable situation. Even if we look at the last two to three decades, gold rates have never gone high in this manner. Today currently gold is at all time high. Now why is this the reason? How are you? Let us understand this a little. Gold can be considered as a safest investment. Gold has always seen this for decades, centuries. Gold is always considered the safest investment because it is easy to cash. It is foreign liquid. However you go to the jewellery, you sell it out. You get cash as compared to the rest of the property. So gold that's why is the on number one priority. Wherever the market is going on right now, should more gold be bought or sold ? Because of the way the market is performing. See, all those who are in the market or even normal people understand that gold has always increased slowly over the past several decades. 1.5 to 2 lakhs, 2 to 2.5 lakhs, 2.5 to 3 lakhs and even if it backfires, even if it is less, then it will be less by 10-15,000. Then it will be fine after going to the place. But from the past, if we look at the rates from August till now, we have seen an increase from Rs 1 lakh to Rs 90,000 and that is a huge move for gold. Now if we talk about the international market, then at this time people are speculating even on silver in the international market. And I would call it speculative because the move they are seeing in silver is approximately a move of $4 to $5. Today, silver is being traded in the local market from 3 to 4000 per tola to 8000 and above at $800 per tola. Meaning, the market has never behaved in this manner, so if someone has it, this is the right time to sell. Meaning it is a great time to sell. Because if he gets into this imagine if he wants to hold on for the long term then it is fine. If he is keeping it only from the trading point of view, then in future gold and other markets will crash because the international stock market is behaving very artificially at this time. The investors of the fish have artificially pumped it up and this scenario can burst at any time in the future. So if you guys are looking from a trading point of view. If you want to play short term trading then this is a great time. If you want to do long term trade and you are thinking that if we buy now and gold will go up, then it is not like that at all. This is the right time for those who are thinking that if we get a good amount then we can keep it, then this is the best time. But after buying, someone is saying that friend, this will go up to Rs 8 lakh to 9 lakh. Ko that is like that is a miserable thing and it is a kind of speculation. Even we do not know which way the market is going to go because if someone places a simple trade at night, then when he wakes up in the morning and sees, the market has crashed if he has not placed a stop loss. I am talking about this from a trading point of view. But if I say in simple words then gold is at all time high. Till date, gold has not made such a big move as it is happening in the market these days. And anyway, by 2025 we are going to see many unimaginable situations. Be it from a political point of view, be it geopolitics, be it from the point of view of the current situation in Pakistan. Let's see what comes next.
